ksa_text: Design and implement NLP systems for defined business tasks, including model selection, data preparation, fine-tuning, evaluation, and deployment documentation.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ensure design and development activities are properly documented (providing a functional description of implementation) and updated as necessary.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of network access control (NAC) systems and software

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Ensure design and development activities are properly documented (providing a functional description of implementation) and updated as necessary.
Knowledge of network access control (NAC) systems and software

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: This task KSA describes execution-level work in AI/ML Engineering that is not expected of a VP Data Products (vp tier). The role is responsible for overseeing and directing this work, not performing it directly. Excluded on execution-seniority grounds.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could real-world VP Data Products practitioners be expected to demonstrate this task KSA in practice, despite the seniority gap?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NewVantage Partners CDO Survey, Wavestone CDAIO Report, and Gartner leadership effectiveness research confirm that vp-tier roles delegate execution responsibilities to appropriate levels rather than owning them directly.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: seniority-based exclusion is consistent with external evidence on vp-tier role expectations. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Execution-level task KSA in AI/ML Engineering is outside the expected competency scope for a VP Data Products (vp tier). The role oversees this work; it does not perform it directly.
